content.subtitle: Place
rows: There are amazing waterfalls **in** the rainforest.
Your passport is **in** the top drawer.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: in a minute / an hour / a year

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: With movement verbs (go, come, walk, run, move, fly), we often use **to** for destination: *go to Japan*.

content.subtitle: Place
rows: She left her glasses **on** the coffee table.
There's a map of the world **on** the wall.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **General vs. Specific**: We say *in the morning/afternoon/evening*, but we use **on** when a specific day is mentioned (**on Monday morning** / **on Wednesday evening** / etc.).
✓ We're flying to Washington in the morning.
✓ We're flying to Washington on Tuesday morning.
**No Preposition**: Do not use a preposition with *tomorrow*, *yesterday*, *tomorrow morning*, *yesterday evening*, etc.
✓ We're flying to Washington tomorrow afternoon.

content.subtitle: Time
rows: The film starts **at** quarter past eight.
Are you visiting your family **at** Easter?

content.subtitle: Place
rows: It gets very cold **at** the top of the mountain.
They used to live **at** 7 Park Avenue.
She must be **at** the gym — she said she was going this afternoon.
He couldn't come to the phone. He was **at** a meeting.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: It is important to compare how we use **in** and **at** for places:
**In**: Used for larger areas that are all around us when we are there. Example: *We spent our holiday in the mountains.*
**At**: Used for smaller places and specific points on a journey. Example: *Can you meet me at the bus stop?*

sections.title: A: Tick if correct; correct if wrong
sections.instructions: If the preposition in bold is correct, type OK. If it is wrong, write the correct preposition.

items.sentence: My sister was born **at** 2012.
The meeting is **in** Friday afternoon.
The concert starts **at** half past eight.
We usually visit our cousins **in** August.
Are you doing anything **at** Sunday?
I prefer reading **on** the evening.
We always have a big meal **at** Christmas.
The school trip is **in** 18th March.

sections.title: B: Complete using on, in or at
sections.instructions: Write on, in or at in each gap.

items.sentence: There were hundreds of fans ...... the stadium.
My grandparents live ...... 25 King Street.
We stayed ...... Madrid for three nights.
Penguins live ...... very cold parts of the world.
There is a beautiful painting ...... the wall.
Did you read the message ...... my notebook?
They spent their holiday ...... Malta.
The children played volleyball ...... the beach.

sections.title: C: Look at the pictures and complete
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with in, on or at.

items.sentence: The picture was taken ...... spring.
They're ...... a football match.
The fish are swimming ...... the river.
The answer is ...... page 38.
The table is ...... the middle of the room.
They're staying ...... a small island.
There's snow ...... the top of the hill.
She's ...... a meeting.

sections.title: D: Complete using words in the box
sections.instructions: Use the words in the box to complete the sentences.

items.sentence: We're planning to move ...... Canada next year.
Would you like to go ...... the cinema tonight?
We stayed ...... a small hotel near the station.
I'll wait ...... the entrance until you arrive.
Come ...... my flat after school.
They were walking ...... the museum when it started raining.
The plane arrived ...... Rome just after midnight.
Please put the forms ...... my desk.
There are cows ...... that field.
How long does it take to fly ...... South America?

sections.title: E: Circle the correct word
sections.instructions: Choose the correct preposition in each sentence.

items.sentence: I'll see you **at / on** the station in ten minutes.
There is a fountain **at / in** front of the museum.
The course begins **in / on** the third of September.
We're visiting my aunt **in / on** Saturday morning.
Is there any milk **at / in** the fridge?
People may live on other planets **at / in** the future.
I'm quite busy **at / on** the moment.
Someone is waiting **at / in** the window.

sections.title: F: Complete the text (Jetlag)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: When you fly ...... a distant country, your body can feel confused.
You might wake up ...... the middle of the night.
Then you feel tired ...... the morning.
Jetlag often happens when you travel ...... a different time zone.
You may leave your home city ...... six o'clock.
Then you fly ...... another continent.
When you arrive ...... the airport, everything seems normal.
Your watch may say it's midnight, but local time could be ...... five in the afternoon.
You arrive ...... the hotel feeling exhausted.
...... night, you may not feel sleepy at all.
Your body still thinks it is ...... your own country.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one wrong preposition. Write the correct one.

items.sentence: They usually go camping **at** summer.
The lesson begins **on** 8:15.
Her birthday is **in** 22nd April.
I left my keys **in** the kitchen table.
The taxi is waiting **on** the door.
We arrived **to** Barcelona in the evening.
The children are playing **in** the beach.
Dinner will be ready **at** ten minutes.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning to the first. Use a maximum of four words.

items.sentence:
The show starts at eight o'clock.
The show doesn't start ...... the morning.

My appointment is on Tuesday afternoon.
My appointment isn't ...... Monday morning.

She is currently at the dentist's.
She is at the dentist's ...... .

We landed in Athens at six o'clock.
We arrived ...... six o'clock.

Let's meet outside the school entrance.
Let's meet ...... the school.

The flowers are in the centre of the garden.
The flowers are ...... the garden.
The bus arrives ...... quarter to seven.
My brother was born ...... 2009.
We're meeting our friends ...... Saturday.
There's a clock ...... the wall.
My cousin lives ...... Australia.
We usually visit our family ...... Easter.
The cat is sleeping ...... the sofa.
They travelled ...... the mountains by car.

sections.title: J: Mixed revision
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one preposition.

items.sentence: We met ...... the library after school.
I keep my phone ...... my school bag.
It gets very hot here ...... the summer.
We're going to a concert ...... Friday night.
There is a small café ...... the corner.
They live ...... 42 River Road.
We are travelling ...... the coast tomorrow.
I found your wallet ...... the drawer.
I'll meet you ...... the bus station.
She'll be here ...... a few seconds.
